key_facts: AGENTS requires workflow routing before action, brownfield context gate, `PROJECT-HANDBOOK.md` and smallest relevant `.specify/project-map/*` reads, and `map-scan -> map-build` when coverage is missing or stale.
Current project-map status files report `freshness: stale`, `dirty: true`, and dirty reasons related to workflow names, workflow contracts, launcher/hooks catalog, and verification entry points.
Before this run, `.specify/project-map/` contained status files but no canonical root or module atlas docs.
Project-map templates require index/root/module layers, capability cards, known unknowns, low-confidence areas, reverse coverage, and actionable operations/testing sections.
Freshness helper scripts require canonical handbook/index/root map files before recording or completing a refresh, and ignore map-state/worker-results runtime files for freshness classification.
recommended_atlas_updates: Generate the full layered atlas from live packet evidence.
Record reverse coverage in map-state and worker-results.
Run complete-refresh only after canonical files exist.
unknowns: The freshness inspection will still classify uncommitted canonical atlas files as changed until these outputs are committed.
